bugs.error_message: User-reported: research/biscuit.out hitches the app - laggy scrollbar and long-lived skeleton placeholders while scrolling.
bugs.root_cause: biscuit.out is 25 MB across only 695 physical lines: 8 single-line records of ~3.2M chars each (+ 4 of ~101K) carrying serialised JSON API responses (`play - Response 200 - OK - {...}` / `FromJSON: {...}`). The whole pipeline assumed sub-KB lines. Added temporary [clog-perf] instrumentation (Rust get_lines timing + a perf_log eprintln sink, JS perf.ts perfMark + longtask observer on fetchPage/highlight/renderLine) and confirmed live: (1) get_lines shipped full text, so PAGE_SIZE=256 pages containing a monster line were 6-13 MB IPC payloads -> fetchPage 370-605ms (Rust build only ~2-4ms; the weight is serialise+transport+JS parse) -> virtual rows had no data -> skeletons. (2) computeHighlights allocates 3x N-length arrays + per-char paint = ~70ms per monster line on first render. (3) longtask 256-271ms when a monster line first renders: ~half highlight, ~half DOM layout of a 3.2M-char `white-space:pre; width:max-content` text node + Vue patch. The cache-key string copy in highlightsFor was sub-1ms (a hypothesis the instrumentation disproved).
bugs.fix: Cap transported line text server-side. build_lines_payload gained a cap param via build_lines_payload_capped(file,start,end,cap); get_lines passes Some(LINE_TEXT_CAP=4096) (char-boundary safe), get_record_lines passes None (full text for the modal). LinePayload/LineRow gained full_len (for the affordance label) and a byte-exact `truncated` bool. NOTE: the first cut inferred truncation UI-side as `full_len(bytes) > text.length(chars)`, which wrongly flagged non-ASCII modal lines (the JSON payloads carry multi-byte chars) so the '+N - show full record' marker appeared inside the full-record modal -- user-caught. Fixed by sending the explicit byte-exact `truncated` flag from build_lines_payload_capped (`text.len() < full_len` after capping) and keying both the affordance and the window-fetch on it; regression test multibyte_line_under_cap_is_not_flagged_truncated. Hit-aware windowing keeps search usable: new get_line_window(file_id,line_index,center,radius) command returns a small slice around a byte offset; tab.ts holds a lineWindows cache + fetchLineWindow/clearLineWindows (cleared on search/rotation/pattern-apply); a LogViewport watcher windows EVERY visible truncated line whose match sits past the cap (each around its own match) so the highlight is visible inline; a match already within the transported head needs no window. Windows are dropped wholesale when the search changes (clearLineWindows in runSearch) AND when the search input is cleared (clearLineWindows in clearSearchState -- the clear-input/Esc path bypasses runSearch, so without this the lines stayed windowed after the highlights vanished), so once a line's highlights disappear it reverts to head truncation (user requirement: windowing tracks the presence of highlights on the line, not the current-hit focus). renderLineSpans renders the window slice with leading/trailing ellipsis 'show full record' affordances (LeafSpan.action='show-record' -> onLeafClick -> fetchAndShowRecord). Truncated non-hit lines show a trailing '... +N MB - show full record' marker (.s-truncation CSS). The full-record modal was rerouted from get_records+get_lines to the uncapped get_record_lines. Net effect: monster-page payloads drop from 6-13 MB to a few KB, highlight/layout no longer see multi-MB text, search matching (Rust-side over full bytes) is unchanged, and every hit stays visible inline. 5 new Rust tests (cap, short-line intact, uncapped path, multibyte-not-truncated, window centre/clamp/out-of-range) + existing pass; clippy/fmt/vue-tsc/vite/vitest all green. The temporary [clog-perf] instrumentation (perf.ts, perf_log command, get_lines timing, perfMark calls) was used to confirm the diagnosis live then stripped before the issue-closing commit.

bugs.root_cause: The OpenWolf daemon crash-looped. The log contains 142,942 identical 8-line startup banners (Scheduled task x5, File watcher started, OpenWolf daemon started, Dashboard server listening on port 18791) and almost no task-execution lines. Early bursts restart ~5x/second (e.g. 07:45:13.905Z then 07:45:14.113Z, ~208ms apart). The crash error itself is never written to daemon.log (the daemon emits its INFO startup banner, then dies on stderr / exits before logging the failure), so the log is pure restart noise. Matches cytostack/openwolf PR #38: `openwolf init`/`update` force-overwrite config.json with hardcoded default ports 18790/18791, so on a host running multiple OpenWolf projects the daemons collide on those ports (EADDRINUSE) and crash-loop. Compounded by OpenWolf having no log rotation or size cap, so the restart banner grows the file unbounded. The tight loop appears to have since settled (recent starts are ~hourly, not 5/sec).
bugs.fix: Not yet applied - daemon.log is gitignored (git check-ignore confirms) so it never reached the repo; this is local disk/noise only. Remediation options: (1) truncate daemon.log to reclaim the 82 MB (safe, ephemeral) - pending user go-ahead, the auto-mode classifier blocked it as out of scope for an investigate request; (2) real fix is PR #38, but npm latest is still 1.0.4 (== installed), so it is merged-but-unreleased and cannot be picked up via `npm update` yet; until it ships, set unique daemon/dashboard ports in each project's .wolf/config.json so multiple projects do not collide on 18790/18791. APPLIED 2026-05-28: moved this project's daemon/dashboard ports from 18790/18791 to 18890/18891 in .wolf/config.json (takes effect on the daemon's next restart). NOTE: cytostack/openwolf is dormant (default-branch last commit 2026-03-20, PR #38 still open/unmerged, npm latest still 1.0.4 == installed) - so `openwolf update` will never ship the #38 fix, but if it were ever run it would clobber config.json back to 18790/18791 and reintroduce the collision.

bugs.error_message: .wolf/anatomy.md collapsed to a 3-file stub (207 -> 88 lines) after editing files during the release
bugs.root_cause: core.autocrlf=true with no .gitattributes let .wolf/*.md working-tree copies silently drift to CRLF (autocrlf hides this: a CRLF working tree compares equal to the LF blob, so git status shows the file clean). OpenWolf's post-write hook (parseAnatomy in .wolf/hooks/shared.js) does content.split("\n") and matches each entry with a regex anchored ...tok\)$. A CRLF line keeps a trailing \r before the \n, so $ never matches and EVERY existing entry is dropped. The hook then re-serializes (LF) keeping only the entries it freshly added for the just-edited file, collapsing anatomy.md to a stub.
bugs.fix: Regenerated with `openwolf scan` (rewrites LF, parses cleanly). Added .gitattributes pinning .wolf/**/*.{md,json,js} to `text eol=lf` so the working tree always matches the parser's expectation, then `git add --renormalize .wolf/` plus a delete+checkout of the still-CRLF cerebrum.md/buglog.json to materialize LF on disk. Upstream openwolf v1.0.4 should split on /\r?\n/ instead -- cannot fix the global npm tool from here, but the .gitattributes removes the trigger.

bugs.error_message: Pressing Space did not expand/collapse a record (a spec feature) in common cases
bugs.root_cause: The Space handler in onDocumentKey toggled stickyHeader.value.lineIndex and bailed when stickyHeader was null. The sticky header only appears when a multi-line record has scrolled partway off the top (its first visible row is a continuation line). When sitting on a collapsed record -- whose only visible row IS its header (line_within_record 0) -- or on any record viewed from its header, stickyHeader is null, so Space no-opped. That is exactly the situation where the user wants to toggle, so Space appeared broken.
bugs.fix: Took three passes. (1) Broadened the target from sticky-header-only to also use the record at the top of the viewport when no sticky header was showing. (2) Made focus reliable: focus the tabindex=0 viewport on mousedown (WebView2 does not always focus it when non-focusable log text is clicked) and allow Space when nothing else holds focus. Neither worked alone. (3) A temporary alert diagnostic at the top of the handler proved the keypress WAS captured (key=' ', inViewport=true, recordIndex populated) but target resolved to a single-line record (recLineCount=1) at the top of the viewport -- not the multi-line record the user had clicked. Root fix: onViewportMouseDown now records the pressed line (pointer Y -> virtual row -> physical line) into lastInteractedLine, and spaceToggleTargetLine returns recordOfLine(lastInteractedLine).record_first_line first, falling back to sticky header then top row. Space now toggles the record the user actually clicked.
bugs.final_behaviour: After the diagnostic, the clicked-line approach worked but was not what the user wanted: Space should fold/unfold the record under the MOUSE CURSOR (hover), not the clicked record. Final implementation: onViewportMouseMove stores the pointer clientY (cleared on mouseleave), and Space maps that Y -> virtual row -> owning record via recordOfLine. No dependence on focus or clicks; Space is left alone when the cursor is outside the log or a text input is focused. A cursor over the sticky-header overlay maps to the top visible row (a continuation of the stuck record), which recordOfLine resolves to that record, so the overlay toggles what it shows.
bugs.lesson: When a UI keypress 'does nothing', instrument before theorising. Two reasoned fixes (dead-zone, focus) missed because the real failure was target resolution picking a single-line record off the top edge -- only a state-dump alert revealed recLineCount=1. Also: confirm the interaction MODEL with the user (hover vs click vs focus) before picking a target heuristic.

bugs.error_message: Search next/prev and marker-rail jumps did not auto-open a collapsed record to reveal the target (the spec requires the containing record to auto-expand). Insights-drawer clicks appeared to work because they target already-open/single-line records.
bugs.root_cause: revealLine in LogViewport.vue gated on lineIsVisible(lineIdx) -- a line-level check. A collapsed record's HEADER line is always visible (it is the single row shown for the folded record), and every caller routinely targets the header: search's hitTargetLine falls back to record_first_line when the buried match line's page is not loaded (and collapsed records never fetch their continuation pages, so it always fell back), and markers/bookmarks sit on the header line. So lineIsVisible returned true and revealLine no-opped, never expanding the record. The existing post-scroll focus retry (bringCurrentHitMatchIntoView) queries the DOM for '.row.is-current-hit .h-search-match', which can never exist while the record is collapsed (continuation rows are not rendered), so it gave up after its frame budget.
bugs.fix: Re-gated revealLine on the RECORD's expanded state via recordExpanded(rec) instead of line visibility: if recordOfLine(lineIdx) is collapsed (multi-line, not already open by mode/manual/transient), open it transiently (single-shot). recordExpanded already honours single-line records, the active mode, and sticky manual expansions, so those correctly no-op. Once the record opens, its continuation rows render and fetch their pages, and the existing scheduleHitFocus retry centres on the real match. Removed the now-unused lineToRow computed and lineIsVisible helper.

bugs.error_message: Tail follow stopped tracking the bottom once a collapse mode was active: new entries appeared but the viewport no longer auto-scrolled to follow them; the user had to scroll down manually
bugs.root_cause: The follow-tail watcher in LogViewport.vue keyed jumpToBottom on props.tab.file.value.line_count. With a collapse mode (or a filter) active the visible bottom is effectiveCount, which is derived from recordIndex -- and recordIndex is refreshed ASYNCHRONOUSLY by refreshRecordIndex() after each tail delta. So on a delta: line_count grew, the watcher fired jumpToBottom and scrolled to effectiveCount-1 while recordIndex was still stale (new record not yet projected), landing at the OLD bottom; then refreshRecordIndex resolved, recordIndex/effectiveCount grew, but no watcher re-fired the jump, so the view stopped following. In plain 'none' mode effectiveCount === line_count synchronously, so follow still worked there -- which is why the regression only surfaced after the global default actually began folding records (see the SettingsPatch fix above).
bugs.fix: Split the watcher: keep the line_count watch solely for scheduleMinimapFetch (minimap is line-indexed), and drive follow off effectiveCount instead -- `watch(effectiveCount, () => { if (followTail) jumpToBottom() })`. effectiveCount is the visible bottom in identity, filter and collapse modes, and it updates when the async recordIndex refresh lands, so the re-jump fires at the right time. A continuation line appended to an already-collapsed bottom record leaves effectiveCount unchanged, so it correctly does not force a jump.

bugs.error_message: Settings -> Behaviour -> 'Collapse records by default' Errors/All options did nothing when clicked; the global default never took effect (per-file modes via the filters popover worked fine)
bugs.root_cause: The collapse-records plan added collapse_records_default to the persisted Settings struct (persistence.rs) but never added it to SettingsPatch in main.rs, the typed struct the update_settings IPC command deserializes the UI patch into. serde ignores unknown fields by default, so the field was silently dropped: update_settings loaded the current Settings, applied none of the collapse value, saved, and returned Settings with the old default 'none'. The UI's reactive settings.value.collapse_records_default therefore never changed, so the viewport's collapseDefault/collapseEffectiveMode stayed 'none'. Per-file worked because it routes through tab.setCollapseMode + the RestoredFile session path, which the plan DID wire.
bugs.fix: Added `pub collapse_records_default: Option<String>` to SettingsPatch and an apply arm `if let Some(v) = patch.collapse_records_default { s.collapse_records_default = v; }` in update_settings (matching the plain `theme` String handling, no validation since the UI only sends none/errors/all and the viewport normalises anything else to 'none'). Added a serde regression test settings_patch_captures_collapse_records_default.

bugs.error_message: The '+N lines' badge on collapsed record headers sat ~0.4rem left of the marker rail, leaving a distracting gap instead of sitting flush against the rail
bugs.root_cause: The .collapse-badge rule in style.css used `right: 0.4rem` for its position: sticky offset. The marker rail is a 20px flex sibling immediately to the right of the .viewport scroll container (which has no right padding), so a 0.4rem right offset pins the badge 0.4rem inside the viewport edge, leaving a visible gap between the badge and the rail. Both surfaces use --bg-viewport so flush would read as one continuous strip.
bugs.fix: Changed .collapse-badge `right: 0.4rem` to `right: 0` so it pins flush to the viewport's right edge (= the marker rail's left edge). Kept margin-left and the ::before left fade.

bugs.error_message: cargo test --workspace fails one test: tests::append_without_newline_then_with_newline_matches_user_repro in crates/clog-app/src/main.rs:3016 panics 'assertion left == right failed: consumed cursor, left: 414, right: 413'
bugs.root_cause: Pre-existing, unrelated to the collapse-records feature (the feature branch never touched main.rs). The test seeds a buffer that is 414 bytes long but asserts the consumed cursor lands at 413 -- an off-by-one in the test's own expected value, not in production code. Discovered while running the Task 13 verification gate for the collapse-records branch.
bugs.fix: NOT FIXED -- out of scope for the collapse-records work and left for a dedicated fix so the change stays isolated. The fix is almost certainly to correct the expected offset from 413 to 414 in the assertion (verify by counting the seed bytes), but the user should confirm whether 414 is the intended cursor before changing the assertion vs. the parser.

bugs.error_message: GitHub issue #3: 'You're on the latest version' toast from the About modal's Check for updates button rendered behind the modal's translucent backdrop, had no auto-dismiss, and the tiny grey-on-grey x was easy to miss
bugs.root_cause: Two layered problems. (1) The toast was a fixed-position element in App.vue with z-index: 10, while BaseModal's backdrop sits at z-index: 200 and DropOverlay at 250 -- any toast spawned while the About modal was open got trapped under the dim overlay. (2) The whole 'toast for an outcome the user explicitly asked for' pattern was wrong: the result of a manual Check for updates belongs next to the trigger, not on the opposite corner of the window.
bugs.fix: Restructured useUpdateBanner.check() to return a CheckOutcome ({ kind: 'available' | 'up-to-date' | 'error' | 'skipped', message? }) instead of pushing into a global toast ref. Removed the App.vue toast element and styles entirely. AboutModal now awaits the inject'd checkForUpdates promise and renders the up-to-date / error message inline as an <output> with an accent-coloured left border (level-error tint on failure), cleared when the modal closes. The 'available' outcome continues to surface via the existing update banner.

bugs.error_message: Minimap indicator handle briefly jumps upward each time new records arrive while tailing, producing a distracting jitter on a live log
bugs.root_cause: When line_count grows, the virtualizer's totalSize updates reactively before the deferred jumpToBottom RAF callback fires. minimapIndicator computes top = scrollTop / total * h with the old scrollTop against the new (larger) total, so the handle slides upward for a frame before the scroll catches up.
bugs.fix: In minimapIndicator, when props.tab.followTail.value is true, pin the handle to the bottom (top = h - height) instead of computing it from scrollTop. Independently improved follow UX: onViewportScroll now auto-enables followTail when the user lands within ROW_HEIGHT of the end via any path (scrollbar, minimap drag, jump-to-bottom button), matching the 'at the end = following' mental model.

bugs.error_message: Toggling the speed rail off and back on via Settings -> Slow requests -> Show speed rail leaves the rail blank after it remounts
bugs.root_cause: The rail is rendered with `v-if="speedRailVisible"`, which destroys the canvas DOM node when the toggle goes off and remounts a fresh blank one when it goes back on. The grid data stays in memory but nothing re-triggers paintSpeedRail on remount -- paintSpeedRail is only called from fetchSpeedGrid / fetchSpeedThresholds / themeObserver, none of which fire on a pure visibility flip.
bugs.fix: Added a `watch(speedRailVisible, ...)` in LogViewport that, when the computed flips to true, schedules a paintSpeedRail() in the next animation frame so Vue has finished re-mounting the canvas and the template ref is populated. Mirrors the pattern used for the minimap heatmap-blend repaint watcher.

bugs.error_message: TailState's head-hash rotation check flagged every append as a rotation when the file was shorter than HEAD_HASH_BYTES
bugs.root_cause: The hash was computed over min(HEAD_HASH_BYTES, current_size) on every poll. Growing the file from N to N+M bytes meant the next poll hashed N+M bytes -- a strictly longer prefix -- so the hash naturally differed and looked like a rotation.
bugs.fix: Pin head_prefix_len at construction (or after a reset_to/rotation) and rehash EXACTLY that prefix length on every subsequent poll. Growth keeps the anchored bytes unchanged so the hash is stable; only a rewrite flips it. Below-prefix-length shrinks are caught by the earlier size-shrink branch.

bugs.error_message: E0117 (orphan rule) when adding impl RecordScanner for Box<dyn RecordScanner + Send + Sync> in clog-app
bugs.root_cause: Both the trait and the Box type live outside clog-app, so the impl is orphan-forbidden. The motivation was to let the tail task hold a runtime-selected scanner across polls without caring whether it was a PatternLayout or a regex.
bugs.fix: Introduced a sized sum type (CompiledScanner { Pattern(CompiledPattern) | Regex(RegexScanner) }) that owns the impl in clog-app. Store ScannerKind (source strings) on OpenedFile and call ScannerKind::compile() each poll to materialise a CompiledScanner. Recompilation is cheap relative to disk I/O.

bugs.error_message: User reported: when detached (follow-tail off), incoming tail appends caused the viewport to visually shift/scroll, so users couldn't stably read a chosen section while tailing remained active.
bugs.root_cause: Two contributing causes. (1) On every Appended delta the UI was calling pages.value.delete(lastPage) followed by an implicit re-fetch via the virtualRows watcher. The delete blanked the cached page synchronously, so visible rows on that page rendered as empty placeholders until the next IPC round trip finished -- visually reading as a shift. (2) No defensive re-anchor of scrollTop across the line_count bump, so any internal virtualizer measurement that happened to nudge scrollTop would not be corrected.
bugs.fix: Replaced delete-then-refetch with an in-place force-refetch (fetchPage gained a `force` flag; cached entries stay rendered until the new payload arrives, then swap atomically via pages.value.set + new Map()). For the detached case, capture scrollTop before the count bump and re-assert it across two requestAnimationFrame ticks (one for the synchronous count change, one for any deferred virtualizer measurement). Follow-tail path still calls jumpToBottom() as before.

bugs.error_message: proptest 'prod_roundtrips' began failing 'must parse as header' on every input after extending BUILTIN_PATTERNS with new entries.
bugs.root_cause: The proptest indexed BUILTIN_PATTERNS by position (BUILTIN_PATTERNS[1].1) to look up the prod pattern. When new patterns were inserted, index 1 no longer pointed at prod, so the test compiled the wrong pattern and rejected every generated prod-shaped line. Same vulnerability existed in the unit tests for `parse_prod_header_no_logger` and `parse_log4j2_default_header`.
bugs.fix: Added a `builtin_pattern(name: &str) -> Option<&'static str>` lookup helper to clog-core::pattern (re-exported from lib.rs) and switched every test (unit + proptest) from BUILTIN_PATTERNS[i] indexing to builtin_pattern("name").unwrap(). The position-stable lookup makes future reordering safe.

bugs.error_message: Uncaught ReferenceError: Cannot access 'effectiveCount' before initialization (at ComputedRefImpl.fn in App.vue) -- app opens blank
bugs.root_cause: In <script setup>, useVirtualizer(computed(() => ({ count: effectiveCount.value, ... }))) was called BEFORE effectiveCount itself was declared. @tanstack/vue-virtual reads the supplied computed synchronously during its setup to initialise internal state, which evaluates the getter immediately. Const declarations are in the temporal dead zone before their initialiser runs, so the getter threw on first access and Vue mount failed before the template ever rendered.
bugs.fix: Move filter-related computeds (filteredLineIndices, effectiveCount, actualLineIndex) ABOVE the useVirtualizer call so they are initialised by the time the virtualizer reads them. lineRowVirtual stays after lineRow since it is only invoked from the template (which runs post-mount). Lesson: in script setup, any computed/function whose value is read synchronously during another setup call must be declared before that call.

bugs.error_message: OpenWolf post-write.js hook silently overwrites .wolf/anatomy.md with a one-file stub on every Edit/Write to a non-.wolf/ file. The destruction looked like a cron firing but cron-state.json showed execution_log: [] -- it was the write hook.
bugs.root_cause: shared.js::parseAnatomy regex demands the strict OpenWolf format: backtick-wrapped filename, em-dash separator ( -- ), and a trailing (~N tok) token suffix. The project's anatomy.md was hand-maintained in plain ASCII per CLAUDE.md's global rule (ASCII hyphen, no token counts), so parseAnatomy returned an empty sections Map. The hook then added only the just-edited file to the empty map and serializeAnatomy wrote a stub with `Files: 1 tracked`. A bare `catch {}` around the whole anatomy-update block hid the parse mismatch. Every non-.wolf/ Edit/Write triggered the same destruction silently.
bugs.fix: Added an 'ASCII-only rule exception' subsection to CLAUDE.md carving .wolf/-managed files out of the global ASCII rule -- they must use the em-dash + (~N tok) format the hook's parser expects. Rebuilt anatomy.md via `openwolf scan` (88 files indexed) so the on-disk format now matches the hook's parser; subsequent edits round-trip cleanly. Future hand-edits to .wolf/anatomy.md must match the canonical format or the hook will drop them on the next write.

bugs.error_message: Dragging from inside a modal input out onto the backdrop and releasing (a normal text-selection gesture) closes the modal.
bugs.root_cause: BaseModal used @click.self on the backdrop. The synthesised click event fires when both mousedown and mouseup happen on the same element OR on certain drag/release sequences; in particular, the click target resolves to the lowest common ancestor of mousedown and mouseup, which is the backdrop when the press starts in a child input and releases on the backdrop. @click.self then matched and closed.
bugs.fix: Replaced backdrop @click.self with paired @mousedown / @mouseup handlers that close only when BOTH events targeted the backdrop element itself. A drag that begins inside any modal child no longer counts as a backdrop click.
